members.name: GetAvailableDisplayRefreshRates
members.summary: Returns display refresh rates supported by the current HMD. Only returned if this feature is supported by the web browser and after the interface has been initialized.

members.name: GetDisplayRefreshRate
members.summary: Returns the display refresh rate for the current HMD. Not supported on all HMDs and browsers. It may not report an accurate value until after using [method set_display_refresh_rate].

members.name: GetInputSourceTargetRayMode
members.summary: Returns the target ray mode for the given [param input_source_id]. This can help interpret the input coming from that input source. See [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRInputSource/targetRayMode]XRInputSource.targetRayMode[/url] for more information.

members.name: GetInputSourceTracker
members.summary: Gets an [XRControllerTracker] for the given [param input_source_id]. In the context of WebXR, an input source can be an advanced VR controller like the Oculus Touch or Index controllers, or even a tap on the screen, a spoken voice command or a button press on the device itself. When a non-traditional input source is used, interpret the position and orientation of the [XRPositionalTracker] as a ray pointing at the object the user wishes to interact with. Use this method to get information about the input source that triggered one of these signals: - [signal selectstart] - [signal select] - [signal selectend] - [signal squeezestart] - [signal squeeze] - [signal squeezestart]

members.name: IsInputSourceActive
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if there is an active input source with the given [param input_source_id].

members.name: IsSessionSupported
members.summary: Checks if the given [param session_mode] is supported by the user's browser. Possible values come from [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRSessionMode]WebXR's XRSessionMode[/url], including: [code]"immersive-vr"[/code], [code]"immersive-ar"[/code], and [code]"inline"[/code]. This method returns nothing, instead it emits the [signal session_supported] signal with the result.

members.name: SetDisplayRefreshRate
members.summary: Sets the display refresh rate for the current HMD. Not supported on all HMDs and browsers. It won't take effect right away until after [signal display_refresh_rate_changed] is emitted.

members.name: EnabledFeatures
members.summary: A comma-separated list of features that were successfully enabled by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. This may include features requested by setting [member required_features] and [member optional_features], and will only be available after [signal session_started] has been emitted. [b]Note:[/b] This may not be support by all web browsers, in which case it will be an empty string.

members.name: OptionalFeatures
members.summary: A comma-seperated list of optional features used by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. If a user's browser or device doesn't support one of the given features, initialization will continue, but you won't be able to use the requested feature. This doesn't have any effect on the interface when already initialized. See the MDN documentation on [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRSystem/requestSession#session_features]WebXR's session features[/url] for a list of possible values.

members.name: ReferenceSpaceType
members.summary: The reference space type (from the list of requested types set in the [member requested_reference_space_types] property), that was ultimately used by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. Possible values come from [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRReferenceSpaceType]WebXR's XRReferenceSpaceType[/url]. If you want to use a particular reference space type, it must be listed in either [member required_features] or [member optional_features].

members.name: RequestedReferenceSpaceTypes
members.summary: A comma-seperated list of reference space types used by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. The reference space types are requested in order, and the first one supported by the user's device or browser will be used. The [member reference_space_type] property contains the reference space type that was ultimately selected. This doesn't have any effect on the interface when already initialized. Possible values come from [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRReferenceSpaceType]WebXR's XRReferenceSpaceType[/url]. If you want to use a particular reference space type, it must be listed in either [member required_features] or [member optional_features].

members.name: RequiredFeatures
members.summary: A comma-seperated list of required features used by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. If a user's browser or device doesn't support one of the given features, initialization will fail and [signal session_failed] will be emitted. This doesn't have any effect on the interface when already initialized. See the MDN documentation on [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRSystem/requestSession#session_features]WebXR's session features[/url] for a list of possible values.

members.name: SessionMode
members.summary: The session mode used by [method XRInterface.initialize] when setting up the WebXR session. This doesn't have any effect on the interface when already initialized. Possible values come from [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRSessionMode]WebXR's XRSessionMode[/url], including: [code]"immersive-vr"[/code], [code]"immersive-ar"[/code], and [code]"inline"[/code].

members.name: VisibilityState
members.summary: Indicates if the WebXR session's imagery is visible to the user. Possible values come from [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRVisibilityState]WebXR's XRVisibilityState[/url], including [code]"hidden"[/code], [code]"visible"[/code], and [code]"visible-blurred"[/code].

signals.name: DisplayRefreshRateChanged
signals.summary: Emitted after the display's refresh rate has changed.

signals.name: ReferenceSpaceReset
signals.summary: Emitted to indicate that the reference space has been reset or reconfigured. When (or whether) this is emitted depends on the user's browser or device, but may include when the user has changed the dimensions of their play space (which you may be able to access via [method XRInterface.get_play_area]) or pressed/held a button to recenter their position. See [url=https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XRReferenceSpace/reset_event]WebXR's XRReferenceSpace reset event[/url] for more information.

signals.name: Select
signals.summary: Emitted after one of the input sources has finished its "primary action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

signals.name: Selectend
signals.summary: Emitted when one of the input sources has finished its "primary action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

signals.name: Selectstart
signals.summary: Emitted when one of the input source has started its "primary action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

signals.name: SessionEnded
signals.summary: Emitted when the user ends the WebXR session (which can be done using UI from the browser or device). At this point, you should do [code]get_viewport().use_xr = false[/code] to instruct Godot to resume rendering to the screen.

signals.name: SessionFailed
signals.summary: Emitted by [method XRInterface.initialize] if the session fails to start. [param message] may optionally contain an error message from WebXR, or an empty string if no message is available.

signals.name: SessionStarted
signals.summary: Emitted by [method XRInterface.initialize] if the session is successfully started. At this point, it's safe to do [code]get_viewport().use_xr = true[/code] to instruct Godot to start rendering to the XR device.

signals.name: SessionSupported
signals.summary: Emitted by [method is_session_supported] to indicate if the given [param session_mode] is supported or not.

signals.name: Squeeze
signals.summary: Emitted after one of the input sources has finished its "primary squeeze action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

signals.name: Squeezeend
signals.summary: Emitted when one of the input sources has finished its "primary squeeze action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

signals.name: Squeezestart
signals.summary: Emitted when one of the input sources has started its "primary squeeze action". Use [method get_input_source_tracker] and [method get_input_source_target_ray_mode] to get more information about the input source.

values.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_GAZE
values.summary: Target ray originates at the viewer's eyes and points in the direction they are looking.

values.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_SCREEN
values.summary: Target ray from touch screen, mouse or other tactile input device.

values.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_TRACKED_POINTER
values.summary: Target ray from a handheld pointer, most likely a VR touch controller.

values.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_UNKNOWN
values.summary: We don't know the target ray mode.

constants.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_UNKNOWN
constants.summary: We don't know the target ray mode.

constants.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_GAZE
constants.summary: Target ray originates at the viewer's eyes and points in the direction they are looking.

constants.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_TRACKED_POINTER
constants.summary: Target ray from a handheld pointer, most likely a VR touch controller.

constants.name: TARGET_RAY_MODE_SCREEN
constants.summary: Target ray from touch screen, mouse or other tactile input device.
